dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7102 IH: Federal Workforce Civics Competency and Accountability Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Hamadeh of Arizona introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform

form.official-title: To amend title 5, United States Code, to require Federal civilian career employees to pass a citizenship test as a condition of employment, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Federal Workforce Civics Competency and Accountability Act.

section.header: Requiring Federal civilian career employees to pass citizenship test as condition of employment
section.text: Subchapter I of chapter 33 of title 5, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following new section (and conforming the table of contents at the beginning of such chapter accordingly):

subsection.text: An individual who fails to pass the citizenship test shall be ineligible for appointment—

paragraph.text: to a position in the competitive service; or

subsection.text: Not later than 1 year after the date of the enactment of the Federal Workforce Civics Competency and Accountability Act, as a condition of continued employment, each individual who is a career employee on such date of enactment shall take the citizenship test.

paragraph.text: As a condition of continued employment, each career employee shall take the citizenship test annually.
If a career employees receives a failing grade on any annual test under paragraph (1), such career employee shall take a citizenship test consisting of all questions on such test.
A career employee may be subject to disciplinary action, up to and including removal, for failing to pass the full citizenship test under paragraph (2).

subsection.text: The citizenship test administered pursuant to—

paragraph.text: subsection (a), (b), or (c)(2) shall consist of all questions on such test; and
subsection (c)(1) shall consist of 20 questions, randomly selected, from the full citizenship test.

subsection.text: An individual shall be deemed to have passed the citizenship test administered pursuant to—

paragraph.text: subsection (a), (b), or (c)(2) if such individual answers at least 90 percent of the questions on the test correctly; and
subsection (c)(1) if such employee answers 15 out of 20 questions on such test correctly.

The Office of Personnel Management, in consultation with the U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services, shall administer the citizenship test under this section, in such form as the Office considers appropriate.
The Office shall establish and maintain a website and other necessary electronic systems to maintain records and other necessary information regarding the citizenship test.

The Office, in consultation with the U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services, shall provide career employees and applicants for employment access to official study materials and online training resources for purposes of taking the citizenship test under this section.
The Office shall submit an annual report to Congress detailing agency compliance rates, test results, and any disciplinary actions taken under this section.
the term career appointee has the meaning given that term in section 3132(a) of this title;

clause.text: an employee occupying a position in the competitive service; and

subparagraph.text: does not include any civil service position that is excepted from the competitive service because of its confidential or policy-making character; and

paragraph.text: the term citizenship test means the test administered for purposes of determining eligibility for naturalization of an alien in accordance with section 312(a)(2) of the Immigration and Nationality Act.
